page: Microsoft Azure E2ds_v4 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the EDSv4 server family. It is provisioned with 2 dedicated vCPUs from a single physical core of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, utilizing Hyper-Threading with 2 threads per core. The system features 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It includes 77 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not feature any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core performance is moderate, with Geekbench single-core scoring 1212.0 and stress-ng single-core scoring 1403.89, both placing in the average category. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are consistently low. The Geekbench multi-core score of 1477.0, stress-ng multi-core score of 1845.78, and PassMark CPU score of 2218.04 all fall into the bottom 10% tier. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only large block bandwidth at 11.88 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 8.93 GB/sec, both ranking in the bottom 10%.

Given the high memory allocation relative to its 2 vCPUs, the E2ds_v4 offers a cost-efficient entry point for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high parallel processing power. It is suitable for small-scale databases, caching layers, or lightweight enterprise applications requiring a larger memory footprint than standard general-purpose instances. However, the poor multi-core performance and low memory bandwidth make it less suitable for compute-heavy tasks, software compilation, or high-throughput web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E2ds_v4 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the EDSv4 family, configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside 77 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1212.0. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics, such as the Geekbench multi-core score of 1477.0 and large-block read bandwidth of 11.88 GB/sec, fall into the bottom 10% tier. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-optimized workloads that do not demand intensive parallel processing. It is suited for small-scale databases, caching systems, and lightweight enterprise applications requiring a larger memory footprint relative to compute resources.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E2ds_v4 is a memory-optimized x86_64 virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 77 GB of local storage, designed for small-scale database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E2ds_v4 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 77 GB of local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine with dedicated Intel Xeon cores and local storage for lightweight, memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 2.6 GHz clock speed
16.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
77 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average single-core performance with average bzip3 decompression
Low multi-core and memory bandwidth performance under heavy parallel loads
Suited for small-scale databases, caching, and memory-heavy lightweight applications
